title: Workshop-Style Feedback Letters
content:
Workshop-Style Feedback Letters

⸻

Karima Karobi – “Filling a Prescription”

Dear Karima,

Thank you for your submission on how to fill a prescription. Your instructions are well-organized and provide a clear, step-by-step breakdown of the process. I especially appreciate how you emphasize the importance of accuracy and safety in handling prescriptions. Your conclusion effectively reinforces this point, making it clear why these steps must be followed carefully.

One area for improvement is the formatting of your steps. Currently, they are listed as bullet points, but using numbered steps could improve readability and help guide the reader more effectively. Additionally, some of the instructions, such as verifying insurance and checking for interactions, could be expanded with brief examples—perhaps mentioning what to do if an insurance claim is denied or how to handle common prescription errors.

Lastly, you might consider adding a brief troubleshooting section to help patients who encounter issues, such as what to do if a prescription is delayed or unavailable. Overall, this is a strong submission with just a few areas that could be refined for even greater clarity and usability.
context: From document: Workshop-Style Feedback Letters — chunk 1 (chunk 1/7)
